title: Chassalia tetrandra
taxonomy: Chassalia tetrandra is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. This family includes over 600 genera and 13,000 species, making it one of the largest families of flowering plants. Chassalia tetrandra, like other members of the family, is a type of flowering plant that produces small, fragrant flowers and often grows in tropical regions. The species Chassalia tetrandra is further classified in the genus Chassalia, which includes several species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The classification of Chassalia tetrandra reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Chassalia tetrandra is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, it is used to treat snakebites and other poisonous bites, as well as to reduce swelling and promote healing. The plant's leaves and roots are often used in teas, infusions, or decoctions to administer its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Chassalia tetrandra as a medicinal plant.

The health benefits of Chassalia tetrandra include its use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
